variants.label: Current generated guidance
variants.notes: Current repo-local startup and router guidance.

variants.label: Minimal static guidance
variants.inline_guidance: Read the project planning wiki before answering project-specific questions. Start with wiki/startup.md and wiki/index.md, then read linked canonical, roadmap, plan, decision, source, or meta pages only when the task needs them. Do not modify files during read-only investigation tasks.
variants.notes: Small static baseline used to compare against the generated guidance surface.

variants.label: Failure-refined candidate
variants.inline_guidance: For Project Librarian tasks, treat wiki/startup.md as compact current context and wiki/index.md as the route map. Before broad repository search, identify the lifecycle area and target wiki directory: canonical for current truth, roadmaps for broad future scope, plans for execution detail, decisions for rationale, sources for external evidence, and meta for wiki operating rules. For code-impact questions, use canonical implementation or code-evidence pages first, then inspect named files. For planning changes, record accepted current truth in a focused canonical page before implementation. Keep read-only benchmark tasks free of file changes and cite exact files used.
variants.notes: Initial candidate distilled from failure modes in guidance-probe planning.
